data.title: HSBC Adjusts Price Target on Morgan Stanley to $117 From $128, Keeps Hold Rating
data.description: Morgan Stanley has an average rating of hold and mean price target of $131, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 0930 GMT on Thursday:

** United Arab Emirates' renewable energy company Masdar said it had fully acquired Greece's Terna Energy <TENr.AT>, after buying a majority stake last year, a further step in its international expansion strategy.

** Synex Business Performance has announced a strategic partnership with Greater Toronto-based FSB Group, the acquirer's first deal since securing investment from Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec and Ares Management.

** Texas-based insurance, financial and HR services firm Higginbotham has acquired Tidwell & Hilburn Insurance in Macon, Georgia, expanding to its 11th office in the state.

data.title: Low Tariff-Impact Sectors Appear Attractive for Euro Credit InvestorsMarket Talk
data.description: Euro credit investors are likely to find attractive valuations in sectors that are less exposed to U.S. tariffs, ING credit strategists say in a note. "As spreads begin to tighten, we prefer segments with lower tariff impact, as they appear equally affordable and are less vulnerable if tariffs are reinstated." Utilities sector credit and telecoms sector credit look attractive as they have less exposure to tariffs, ING says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: INTERVIEW - Fark Labs looks for startup investment opportunities in W. Balkans
data.description:
ANKARA (Turkey), April 10 (SeeNews) - Türkiye-based global innovation and transformation hub Fark Labs said it is looking for investment opportunities across Southeastern Europe and in the Western Balkans in particular, and could invest in the region as early as next year, its chief innovation officer Omer Hantal said.

"This year we are still looking for possibilities, especially in the Western Balkans. There are quite catchy things for the startup investment for sure. Now we are reforming our funding system and also our own funds and afterwards we will restart to look for the countries nearby, such as North Macedonia, Bosnia, Albania, Serbia and Moldova," Omer Hantal told SeeNews on the sidelines of the European Innovation Council (EIC) summit held in Brussels last week.

EIC is the European Union’s flagship innovation programme to identify, develop and scale up breakthrough technologies and game-changing innovations.

Hantal is also an angel investor and the community officer in Türkiye of The European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT), a body of the European Union and Europe’s largest network strengthening innovation, which was set up in 2008 to create jobs and deliver sustainable and smart growth. EIT is an integral part of Horizon Europe, the EU’s framework programme for research and innovation.

Fark Labs is an innovation hub with over 70 startups investments and six incubator startups, and it is working with lots of Horizon projects and EIT.

"In the last four or five years, we are doing lots of contribution to European innovation ecosystem from Türkiye, but also as we have operations in Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia and so on, we are contributing lots of ecosystems all around Europe as well," Hantal said.

Fark Labs has invested more than $15 million (13.5 million euro) in 70 startups in Türkiye and all around the globe. Along with Türkiye, it has also invested in startups worldwide, from the United States and Israel to Germany and Estonia but so far it has not invested in startups in Southeastern Europe yet.

Hantal went on to say that Fark Labs’s strategic investments can go up to 3 million euro, like an investment it has made in automotive production. For incubated projects, the value of its investment is up to 200,000 euro for two years in which it is providing support. For other investments, its individual contribution can range from 50,000 euro to half a million euro as a venture capital ticket.

Hantal noted that Fark Labs invests only in minority stakes in startups.

"The largest investment that we have on the startup side is up to 24%, which is a strategic investment to become a board member and to be together with the founders. But while we are investing, we prefer to invest into the dream of the startup founders," he added.

Fark Labs is currently reforming its founding mechanisms and probably from next year it would will start to look for more investment opportunities outside Türkiye. This year it has a call, named IndustX Innovation Call, offering industrial tech startup around Türkiye to scale their businesses with top industry partners and growth opportunities.

($ = 0.903 euro)

data.title: Šiaulių Bankas Seeks ECB's Approval for Share Buyback
data.description:
Šiaulių Bankas (SAB1L.VS) filed an application with the European Central Bank, or ECB, to repurchase 4.5 million shares, according to a Thursday release.

This follows an approval granted by ECB in August 2024 for the repurchase of 13,745,114 shares, with 2,652,251 shares remaining under the available limit.

The Lithuania-based commercial bank plans to fully utilize the available limit no earlier than the payment date for its 2024 dividends, set for April 25, 2025.

data.title: Signature Transportation Services Launches Signature Elite Charters and Signature Elite Tours, Expanding Group and Student Travel Services
data.description:
Signature Transportation Services introduces Signature Elite Charters and Signature Elite Tours, offering enhanced charter and student group travel solutions in the Nashville area.

Nashville, Tennessee--(Newsfile Corp. - April 10, 2025) - Signature Transportation Services, a long-established provider of luxury chauffeured services based in Nashville, has announced the launch of two specialized divisions: Signature Elite Charters and Signature Elite Tours. This strategic expansion strengthens the company's ability to meet the growing regional demand for group transportation and customized student travel experiences.

Signature Elite Charters is a new chapter in the company's evolution, offering full-service charter bus solutions for corporate events, athletics, and other group needs. This division was created in partnership with Alan Wise, founder of Wise Coaches and former president of the Tennessee Motorcoach Association. The venture combines Wise's decades of industry expertise with the service standards of Signature Transportation Services.

Signature Transportation Services Launches Signature Elite Charters and Signature Elite Tours

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10740/248065_fbe10f67e42f1b20_001full.jpg

"Signature Elite Charters is an opportunity to elevate what group mobility means in Middle Tennessee," said Michael Dozier, majority owner of Signature Transportation Services. "Alan Wise brings a proven legacy of operational excellence, and we are excited to launch this new phase with him."

The charter fleet includes high-capacity vehicles such as Sprinters, mini buses, and full-size coaches, all operating with an emphasis on safety, reliability, and a high standard of Nashville Bus Charter service.

Meanwhile, Signature Elite Tours focuses on educational and performance-based travel. The company creates Nashville Tours with custom itineraries for student and adult groups, including bands, orchestras, school trips, and more. The team, led by seasoned professionals Linda Fleming and Susan Fitzsimmons, offers a hands-on approach to planning trips that are both enriching and logistically sound.

With nearly 70 years of combined experience, Fleming and Fitzsimmons provide groups with expert guidance and logistical support-from securing hotels and transportation to scheduling performances and educational experiences. The goal is to ensure every group receives a unique, thoughtfully built travel program that meets budget and academic standards.

Signature Transportation Services has announced the launch of two specialized divisions

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10740/248065_fbe10f67e42f1b20_002full.jpg

Signature Elite Tours distinguishes itself by offering personalized service rather than pre-packaged itineraries. "No two groups are the same," said Susan Fitzsimmons, Certified Tour Professional. "We build each experience from the ground up, ensuring every aspect aligns with the group's purpose."

These new divisions reinforce Signature Transportation Services' broader mission: to deliver high-quality, reliable, and innovative chauffeured experiences. The addition of Signature Elite Charters and Signature Elite Tours reflects the company's commitment to meeting the evolving needs of its clients while continuing to grow as a leader in the transportation industry.

About Signature Transportation Services:

Founded in January 2001, Signature Transportation Services is based in Nashville, Tennessee. With a diverse fleet that includes luxury sedans, SUVs, Sprinters, vans, mini buses, and motor coaches, the company offers high-level chauffeured services for individual and group needs. Signature remains focused on integrity, service, and innovation, delivering reliable transportation experiences with every ride.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10740/248065_fbe10f67e42f1b20_003full.jpg

data.title: Trump's stance against Nippon bid sends U.S. Steel shares plunging
data.description:
** U.S. Steel's shares fall 11.4% to $40 premarket

** Trump says he does not want to see U.S. Steel go to Japan

** The U.S. President had earlier ordered a fresh review on Nippon Steel's bid for U.S. Steel to help determine if "further action" is appropriate

** At the same time, activist investor Ancora says it withdraws its nomination of director candidates at U.S. Steel

** U.S. Steel holds a trailing 12 months PE ratio of 20.99 vs. Industry median of -4.33

** According to data compiled by LSEG, 7.15% of X's float was sold short as of March 31, 2025

** 6 of 11 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher and 5 "hold"; their median PT is $41.5

** YTD, stock has risen 32.8% vs. 15.3% fall in the benchmark S&P 500 index

data.title: German Economic Institutes Slash Growth Forecast for 2025
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Germany's economic output will nearly stagnate this year, with U.S. tariffs in the short term dragging on the Europe's largest economy.

A closely watched twice-yearly survey of leading economic research institutes expects Germany's economy to expand just 0.1% this year, down from the 0.8% expected in September 2024.

Germany's gross domestic product declined in both of the last two years, as high energy prices prompted by Russia's invasion of Ukraine led to a protracted slump in its key industrial sector.

The president of Germany's Bundesbank, Joachim Nagel, has already warned that Germany's economy could hardly grow this year, and that tariffs could threaten the country with a third year of recession.

"The geopolitical tensions and the protectionist trade policy of the U.S. are exacerbating the already tense economic situation in Germany," said Torsten Schmidt, head of economic research at the RWI Leibniz Institute for Economic Research, one of institutes that compiles the forecast.

Specific effects of the tariffs are, however, difficult to quantify, the institutes said, as tariff rates have never been raised so sharply in the current globalized economy.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Yamada Consulting Group Raises Earnings, Dividends Forecasts
data.description:
Yamada Consulting Group raised its full-year forecasts due to strong M&A advisory and management consulting business, a major real estate deal, and contributions from its newly acquired company, Pinnacle, according to a Wednesday Tokyo bourse filing.

The consulting firm now expects an attributable profit of 2.92 billion yen, higher than the previous estimate of 2.87 billion yen, and an earning per share of 153.04 yen, up from the initial projection of 150.43 yen.

Yamada also expects sales to come in at 22.7 billion yen, higher than the 21.1 billion yen previously estimated.

The company also projects to pay a year-end dividend of 39 yen, up from the initial projection of 38 yen, the filing said.

Yamada Consulting's shares gained nearly 7% at market close.

data.title: Will Trump really achieve his goals through tariffs?
data.description:
During his presidential campaign, Donald Trump promised to spark an economic boom and rekindle faith in the American dream. Such a goal sounds more than admirable for the country and the markets. Indeed, the S&P 500 and other indexes initially rallied, hoping this vision would become a reality. The U.S. dollar index also began the year strong, trading above the 110 level, marking its highest point since November 2022. But the closer we got to its execution, the clearer it became that it would not be so simple.

As part of the measures being taken, on April 2, the U.S. president imposed tariffs on goods from 211 countries and territories. In some cases, the tariffs were as high as 50%, and if additional measures against China are implemented, that figure could rise even higher. Trump framed the move as a way to rebuild the U.S. economy, calling it a “declaration of economic independence.”

At least in theory, tariffs are supposed to bring factories back to the U.S. and draw in new investment. However, any meaningful impact would take time, and there’s no guarantee that companies will be eager to set up shop in the U.S. — especially with figures like Macron urging EU firms to freeze their investments in America. In the meantime, primarily U.S. consumers will end up shouldering the extra costs tied to imports.

As for why, the logic is simple: when an imported product enters the U.S., the U.S. importer pays a tax to the government based on the product's country of origin. That cost is passed directly on to the final price of the product. So, in the end, it is the buyer — often a U.S. citizen — who foots the bill.

And if the overall objective is to alleviate the federal budget deficit, the math still doesn't add up. Estimates suggest the new tariffs could bring in between $400 billion and $600 billion. But last year's budget deficit reached $1.9 trillion. Let's imagine that Trump also follows through with his proposed tax cuts.

His government has proposed eliminating the income tax for those earning less than $150,000 a year. That single measure could cost the federal budget about $10 trillion by 2035. If payroll taxes are also eliminated, the total could rise to $15 trillion. That's between $1 trillion and $1.5 trillion annually, enough to significantly increase the national debt.

So, instead of boosting the economy or tackling the debt, we might end up with the opposite. The administration seems to understand this, which is why there’s still hope that at least some of the tariff policies will be rolled back or reworked (50+ countries are already in talks with the U.S.). As for the proposal to eliminate taxes for those earning under $150,000, there’s a chance that threshold could be cut by at least half.

data.title: Japanese copper smelters' H1 output seen falling 1.3% y/y
data.description:
Japan's refined copper production in the first half of the 2025/26 financial year is expected to fall 1.3% year-on-year, Reuters calculations from plans outlined by smelters showed, as soft demand for wire and cable offsets firmer demand for copper alloy products.

The first half of the financial year runs from April to September.

Japan's largest copper supplier, Pan Pacific Copper (PPC), expects its first-half supply to remain nearly flat from a year earlier, with no scheduled maintenance, a company spokesperson said.

PPC, jointly owned by JX Advanced Metals , Mitsui Mining and Smelting and Marubeni , outsources smelting and refining operations to its parent companies' plants. It procures raw materials and sells refined metals.

PPC expects domestic demand to stay in line with the previous year, as construction and electric wire demand remains weak due to labour shortages. Additionally, slow housing starts will weigh on copper alloy product demand, the spokesperson added.

In March, the Japan Copper and Brass Association projected a 5.4% increase in domestic demand for copper alloy products in the 2025/26 financial year, which started this month, citing a recovery in the chip sector and slight growth in the auto sector.

The Japan Electric Wire & Cable Makers' Association forecast a 0.4% decline in domestic copper wire and cable shipments for the current year, as stronger demand for electric power and exports will be countered by a drop in telecommunications.

In December, Toho Zinc announced business revitalization plans, under which it will suspend major facilities at its Annaka Smelter and Refinery, north of Tokyo, by the end of March 2025, effectively exiting the zinc smelting business.

Below are the production plans of base metals in metric tons for October to March by key suppliers PPC, Sumitomo Metal Mining (SMM) , Mitsubishi Materials , Nittetsu Mining , Furukawa , Dowa Holdings , Mitsui Mining and Toho Zinc.

data.title: Mitsui & Co Invests $2B in US Clean Ammonia Subsidiaries
data.description:
Mitsui & Co will inject an additional $1 billion each into its US-based subsidiaries, MIT Clean Energy and MIT Clean Ammonia, to fund the Blue Point low-carbon ammonia project, bringing their capital to 10% of Mitsui's total.

Both Delaware-incorporated entities will receive phased funding through 2029 to develop ammonia production capacity targeting decarbonization markets, according to a Wednesday bourse filing.

The investment follows Mitsui's final commitment to the Texas-based project, which aims to supply 1 million tons annually of low-carbon ammonia for power and shipping.

Mitsui & Co's shares gained nearly 8% at market close.

data.title: Market Chatter: Trump Says TikTok Deal Still 'On the Table'
data.description:
US President Donald Trump said the potential sale of TikTok is still "on the table", Reuters reported Wednesday.

The deadline for the deal with TikTok's parent, ByteDance, was extended to June 19. The short video platform will be banned from the US if no deal is made by that date.

Trump said while there are "very good people" considering the deal, they have to "wait and see what's going to happen with China", the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Fortuna Mining Reports Lower Q1 Gold Equivalent Production
data.description:
Fortuna Mining reported Thursday that it produced 103,459 gold equivalent ounces in Q1, down from 112,543 ounces a year earlier.

For 2025, the company said it continues to expect production of 380,000 to 422,000 gold equivalent ounces.

data.title: Mongolia Inflation Rate Slows in March
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Mongolia slowed to 9.1% in March 2025 from an almost one-year high of 9.6% in the previous month.

Prices moderated mainly for food and non-alcoholic beverages (8.2% vs 9.3% in February), transport (4.7% vs 8%), and health (4.1% vs 4.7%).

Meanwhile, inflation picked up slightly for restaurants and hotels (14.6% vs 14.3%) and miscellaneous goods and services (7.4% vs 7.3%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.3% in March, easing from a 1.4% increase in the preceding period.
